page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-8 is a virtual machine instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The instance is configured with 30.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, and features zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

This instance utilizes dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring consistent processing capabilities without resource sharing or CPU credit limitations. While benchmark data is not available for this specific configuration, the hardware profile represents a balanced resource density. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and standard memory ratio position this instance for predictable workloads where resource contention must be avoided.

The c4n-standard-8 is designed for workloads requiring moderate compute and memory resources. Suitable applications include web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and general-purpose business applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.75 GB of RAM per core. This instance features dedicated CPU allocation to ensure predictable performance without resource contention. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Lacking local storage and GPU acceleration, the instance is designed for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and standard CPU processing. The c4n-standard-8 is suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, containerized microservices, and application servers that require a balanced ratio of compute to memory resources without the need for specialized hardware accelerators.
